name: ExtensionExampleScenario_Instance_Content
title: R5: Example instance data (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ExampleScenario.instance.content` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ExampleScenario.instance.content` 0..1 `Reference`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ExampleScenario.instance.content` 0..1 `Reference`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ExampleScenario.instance.content` has a context of ExampleScenario.instance based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`.
Element `ExampleScenario.instance.content` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `ExampleScenario.instance.content` has a context of ExampleScenario.instance based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ExampleScenario`.
Element `ExampleScenario.instance.content` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If not conveying FHIR data or not using the same version of FHIR as this ExampleScenario instance, the reference must be to a Binary.

element.definition: Points to an instance (typically an example) that shows the data that would corespond to this instance.
element.comment: If not conveying FHIR data or not using the same version of FHIR as this ExampleScenario instance, the reference must be to a Binary.
